faqs.q: What are the three core parts of executive function?
faqs.a: Researchers usually split executive function into working memory, inhibition, and cognitive flexibility. Together these skills let you plan, focus, switch tasks, and resist distraction.

faqs.q: Is poor executive function a sign of low intelligence or laziness?
faqs.a: No. Executive function isn't intelligence, motivation, or character. A person can be smart, motivated, and high-effort and still struggle with these specific, separable skills.
It can be temporarily weakened by sleep loss, stress, depression, anxiety, certain medications, and substance use. Many mental health conditions, including ADHD, also affect it.
